differential_diagnoses: Streptococcal pneumonia (S. pneumoniae or S. pyogenes)
Rhabdomyolysis induced by herbal supplements (e.g., burdock root toxicity)
next_steps: Initiate urgent hemodialysis for severe acute kidney injury, hyperkalemia, and acidosis
Obtain sputum culture and gram stain
Perform Bronchoalveolar Lavage (BAL) for microbial culture and PCR (Legionella, viruses, fungi)
Serum and urine toxicology screen for heavy metals and specific toxins (aristolochic acid)
Creatine kinase monitoring and aggressive fluid resuscitation
Consider muscle biopsy if rhabdomyolysis persists without clear etiology

differential_diagnoses: Severe community-acquired pneumonia with secondary rhabdomyolysis
Toxin-induced rhabdomyolysis with secondary respiratory distress
next_steps: Initiate emergent hemodialysis for refractory hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage for PCR/culture including Legionella and viral panel
Monitor troponin and perform echocardiogram to rule out myocarditis
Aggressive fluid resuscitation with monitoring of strict intake and output
Consult Infectious Disease and Nephrology specialists
Review patient for specific environmental exposures: recent potting soil, hot tubs, or building water system issues
